name: Listing QC Tool (Internal Web App)
bullets: Built an internal web app to quickly review and fix real-estate listing data inconsistencies (e.g., wrong community/builder, missing values, or mismatched fields across listings).

Designed the workflow around listing “clusters” (groups of records likely referring to the same place/community/builder): operators pick a company and ZIP code, then review clusters and standardize fields across many listings.
Implemented speed-focused editing: open a cluster to compare all listings, apply common fixes quickly, and perform bulk edits across many listings at once.
Added guardrails for accuracy: a QC Log records changes and supports an undo flow to revert a past change when needed.
Improved operator efficiency with helpers like deterministic sorting for easier scanning and copy/paste prompt generation that reflects the exact listings currently under review.
Extended the backend in a backwards-compatible way to support correct undo behavior (e.g., clearing fields to NULL) without requiring schema changes.
